findings: SMR deployment success depends on achieving economic viability, robust safety and security, environmental sustainability, public acceptance, and regulatory certainty — all of which are interconnected and influence each other.
Economic risks — including high capital costs, uncertain operating costs, and difficulty attracting financing for FOAK projects — are a major hurdle for SMR deployment.
Extended licensing and permitting timelines substantially increase SMR project costs and delay deployment, representing a critical regulatory risk.
The absence of international harmonization in regulatory standards creates barriers to cross-border SMR deployment and adds to project risk.
Supply chain weaknesses — including insufficient domestic manufacturing capabilities, supplier qualification gaps, and complex logistics for large modules — pose significant risks to SMR deployment.
